title: hieracium vogesiacum subsp. chrysanthemoides
intro: Hieracium vogesiacum subsp. chrysanthemoides is a lesser-known subspecies of the Hieracium genus, commonly referred to as hawkweed, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Hieracium vogesiacum subsp. chrysanthemoides include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, or topical applications, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
